_provenance.discipline: Process-not-people. 3R-Critical: Royalty is structural to the story. We do NOT characterise the Agong's preferences, motivations, or likely future conduct. Final synthesis applies (a) Stage 2's OVERCLAIM flag on the reframe card omitting Article 150(3); (b) Stage 3's corrections on Teh Cheng Poh overcompression, '60 years' arithmetic, 'suspended Parliament' legal loose wording, 'policy-centric' quotation framing, and 'architecture is sealed' overclaim; (c) Stage 3's resolved needs_verification items (HC judge = Wan Ahmad Farid bin Wan Salleh; Act A514 in force 15 May 1981). Stage 4 (retired) MY_REASONING perspective expansions NOT imported — would inflate length past wordy cap and breach 3R discipline. Stage 5 (retired) VERIFIED structural-silence attacks on Conference of Rulers as unelected/closed-session NOT imported — explicitly breaches 3R Royalty discipline. Stage 5's audience-check critique noted in synthesis_notes but not actioned because tighter constitutional precision is the issue's editorial purpose.

headline: Court of Appeal: no court can review an emergency call — issuance or refusal
context: On 4 May 2026 the Court of Appeal dismissed lawyer Syed Iskandar Syed Jaafar's challenge to the Agong's October 2020 refusal of PM Muhyiddin's emergency request. The panel held Article 150(8) — inserted by Act A514 in 1981 — ousts review of both issuance and refusal.

cards.big: Court of Appeal: the Agong's emergency call — issue it, or refuse it — is final. No court may ask why.
cards.sub: The challenge was to a refusal (October 2020). The same architecture covers issuance — under which Parliament did not sit for seven months in 2021.
sources: Court of Appeal Grounds of Decision, Syed Iskandar bin Syed Jaafar v Kerajaan Malaysia & Ors, W-01(A)-178-03/2024, dated 4 May 2026 (Stage 3 verified, e-Kehakiman): https://efs.kehakiman.gov.my/EFSWeb/DocDownloader.aspx?DocumentID=28abf4f0-752d-476d-8a2f-ad2b03f6e3a5&Inline=true

Free Malaysia Today, 'Emergency declarations beyond courts' jurisdiction, rules Court of Appeal', 4 May 2026 (Stage 2/3 verified): https://www.freemalaysiatoday.com/category/nation/2026/05/04/emergency-declarations-beyond-courts-jurisdiction-rules-court-of-appeal

The Star, 'Appeals court dismisses appeal over King's refusal to declare emergency', 4 May 2026: https://www.thestar.com.my/news/nation/2026/05/04/appeals-court-dismisses-appeal-over-king039s-refusal-to-declare-emergency

Emergency (Essential Powers) Ordinance 2021, P.U.(A) 12/2021, section 14 — displaced ordinary constitutional rules on summoning, proroguing and dissolving Parliament (Stage 3 verified): https://assets.theedgemarkets.com/pdf/pua_20210114_PUA12-Emergency%20%28Essential%20Powers%29%20Ordinance%202021.pdf
Wikipedia, '2021 Malaysian state of emergency' — nationwide 11 Jan 2021 to 1 Aug 2021, Sarawak extended: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2021_Malaysian_state_of_emergency

cards.big: Article 150(8): the Agong's satisfaction 'shall be final and conclusive and shall not be challenged or called in question in any court on any ground.'
cards.sub: Parliament added 150(8) and (9) by Act A514 in May 1981, to oust review of emergencies after the Privy Council's 1979 Teh Cheng Poh ruling.
sources: Federal Constitution of Malaysia, Article 150(8) — primary text via Attorney General's Chambers (Stage 3 verified): https://lom.agc.gov.my/federal-constitution.php

Federal Constitution notes: Article 150(8) and (9) added by Act A514, paragraph 15(d), in force from 15 May 1981 (Stage 3 verified via SPRM PDF copy): https://www.sprm.gov.my/admin/files/sprm/assets/pdf/penguatkuasaan/perlembagaan-persekutuan-bi.pdf

Teh Cheng Poh alias Char Mer v Public Prosecutor [1979] 2 WLR 623 (PC); Privy Council judgment held emergency ordinances reviewable when Parliament was sitting (Stage 3 verified): https://www.casemine.com/judgement/uk/5b2897cf2c94e06b9e19b8fe

Court of Appeal Grounds of Decision, 4 May 2026, paras [109]-[113] — confirms Act A514 added 150(8) to exclude court review and 150(9) to address Teh Cheng Poh's ordinance-making reasoning (Stage 3 verified).

cards.big: In 2018 the Federal Court ruled judicial review is part of the Constitution's basic structure — and ouster clauses cannot remove it (Indira Gandhi).
cards.sub: The 4 May panel accepted the doctrine — but said emergency is policy-centric (security, foreign relations, public order) and outside court competence.
sources: Indira Gandhi Mutho v Pengarah Jabatan Agama Islam Perak & Ors [2018] 1 MLJ 545 (FC) — Federal Court held judicial review is 'essential to the constitutional role of the courts, and inherent in the basic structure of the Constitution' (Stage 3 verified, cited as [2018] 3 CLJ 145): https://www.deolgill.com/panel/upload/resources/Federal%20Court%20-%20Indira%20Gandhi.pdf

Cambridge Asian Journal of Comparative Law, 'A Contextual Approach to Unconstitutional Constitutional Amendments': https://www.cambridge.org/core/journals/asian-journal-of-comparative-law/article/contextual-approach-to-unconstitutional-constitutional-amendments-judicial-power-and-the-basic-structure-doctrine-in-malaysia/8F559E8EC69189C4AF1998C7D8050862

Court of Appeal Grounds of Decision, 4 May 2026, paras [94], [117]-[120] — no contradiction between basic structure and ouster clauses in emergency context; courts ill-equipped to assess national security and sensitive executive information (Stage 3 verified). 'Policy-centric' used as paraphrase from FMT and Star reporting, NOT presented as direct judicial quote per Stage 3 correction.

cards.big: Five federal emergencies since 1964 (1964, 1966, 1969, 1977, 2021). One documented refusal on record: October 2020.
cards.sub: That refusal came after the Agong met seven Malay Rulers at Istana Negara on 25 October 2020. With courts now out, it is the only documented refusal the system has produced.
sources: BERNAMA, 'Chronology of state of emergency in Malaysia' — five federal proclamations: 1964 (Konfrontasi), 1966 (Sarawak), 1969 (May 13), 1977 (Kelantan), 2021 (COVID-19): https://www.bernama.com/en/news.php?id=1921010
The Vibes, 'Chronology of state of emergency proclamations in Malaysia': https://www.thevibes.com/articles/news/13515/chronology-of-state-of-emergency-proclamationsin-malaysia
Malaysiakini, 'Declaration of emergency in our history': https://www.malaysiakini.com/news/547827
Court of Appeal Grounds of Decision, 4 May 2026, para [3] — confirms October 2020 refusal sequence (Stage 3 verified).

Malay Mail, 'Agong says no need for a state of emergency', 25 October 2020 — Istana Negara meeting with the Agong and seven other Malay Rulers (Sultans of Johor, Perak, Kedah, Terengganu, Negeri Sembilan, Perlis, Selangor): https://www.malaymail.com/news/malaysia/2020/10/25/agong-says-no-need-for-a-state-of-emergency/1916182
BenarNews, 'Malaysian King Rejects PM's Request to Declare Emergency', 25 October 2020 — corroborating: https://www.benarnews.org/english/news/malaysian/king-politics-emergency-10252020092225.html

cards.big: Two checks survive in text: Parliament's Art. 150(3) annulment, and the Conference of Rulers. In 2021, Parliament did not sit while the emergency ran.
sources: Federal Constitution Article 150(3) — Proclamation of Emergency and any ordinance under Article 150(2B) shall be laid before both Houses of Parliament, may cease to have effect if both Houses pass annulment resolutions (Stage 2 + Stage 3 flagged as omitted; primary text via SPRM PDF copy): https://www.sprm.gov.my/admin/files/sprm/assets/pdf/penguatkuasaan/perlembagaan-persekutuan-bi.pdf

Emergency (Essential Powers) Ordinance 2021, P.U.(A) 12/2021, section 14 — Parliament summoned, prorogued and dissolved on dates the Yang di-Pertuan Agong thought appropriate during emergency; in effect, Parliament did not sit from January to July 2021: https://assets.theedgemarkets.com/pdf/pua_20210114_PUA12-Emergency%20%28Essential%20Powers%29%20Ordinance%202021.pdf
Federal Constitution Article 38 — Conference of Rulers as constitutional body.
Malay Mail, 'Agong says no need for a state of emergency', 25 October 2020 — documented mechanism of the 2020 refusal.

cards.big: Like a referee whose call and non-call both stand — with no replay rule on either.
cards.sub: The Agong issued the 2021 emergency on Cabinet advice. He refused the 2020 request despite Cabinet advice. The 4 May ruling closes the courtroom on both.
sources: Court of Appeal Grounds of Decision, 4 May 2026, paras [2], [3], [10], [11] — frames 2020 issue as advice of PM or Federal Cabinet; para [10] states the 2021 proclamation followed PM's advice (Stage 3 verified).
Editorial analogy mirroring the dual-direction effect of Article 150(8) as re-affirmed 4 May 2026.

cards.big: 1979 Privy Council: reviewable. 1981 Parliament: ousted. 2018 Federal Court: ouster invalid. 2026 Court of Appeal: emergencies excluded. Only political checks remain.
sources: Editorial synthesis tracing the verified chronology: Teh Cheng Poh [1979] 2 WLR 623 (PC); Constitution (Amendment) Act 1981 (Act A514) inserting Article 150(8) and (9); Indira Gandhi [2018] 1 MLJ 545 (FC) holding judicial review part of the Constitution's basic structure; Court of Appeal 4 May 2026 (Supang Lian, Collin Lawrence Sequerah and Alwi Bin Haji Abdul Wahab JJCA) carving emergency proclamations out of the basic-structure protection as a policy-competence category.

Federal Constitution Article 150(3) preserves a parliamentary annulment check; the 4 May 2026 ruling is the Court of Appeal's current position unless disturbed by the Federal Court or constitutional amendment (Stage 3 hierarchy/temporal caveat applied to soften 'architecture is sealed' to 'only political checks remain').

Free Malaysia Today, The Star, Malay Mail, BenarNews, Time, BERNAMA, Malaysiakini, The Vibes, Reuters — plus primary constitutional and case-law sources (Federal Constitution Articles 150(3), 150(8), 150(9) via SPRM/AGC; Court of Appeal Grounds of Decision 4 May 2026 via e-Kehakiman; Teh Cheng Poh [1979] 2 WLR 623 via Casemine; Indira Gandhi [2018] 1 MLJ 545 via Cambridge AJCL analysis and deolgill.com judgment text; Emergency (Essential Powers) Ordinance 2021 P.U.(A) 12/2021 via theedgemarkets.com archive of the gazetted text), academic and practice analysis (Malaysian Public Law, Lee Hishammuddin Allen & Gledhill via Lexology, Fulcrum.sg / ISEAS-Yusof Ishak Institute, Joshua Wu Kai-Ming legal blog, NUS Singapore Journal of Legal Studies 1968 on Stephen Kalong Ningkan)
confidence: High on the 4 May 2026 ruling outcome, panel composition (now sourced to Court of Appeal Grounds via e-Kehakiman PDF; full names Supang Lian, Collin Lawrence Sequerah, Alwi Bin Haji Abdul Wahab), and Article 150(8) text (AGC/SPRM primary). High on Act A514's insertion of 150(8) and 150(9) on 15 May 1981 (Stage 3 verified). High on Teh Cheng Poh [1979] 2 WLR 623 holding that emergency ordinances were reviewable when Parliament was sitting (Stage 3 verified). High on Indira Gandhi [2018] 1 MLJ 545 basic-structure holding (Stage 3 verified). High on the five-emergencies chronology (Stage 3 confirmed with arithmetic note: '60 years' replaced with 'since 1964'). High on the 25 October 2020 Istana Negara meeting (Agong + 7 Malay Rulers). High on Article 150(3) parliamentary-annulment text (Stage 2/3 flagged omission; now sourced to SPRM PDF copy). High on Emergency Ordinance 2021 section 14 (Stage 3 verified): Parliament did not sit during the emergency. Medium on Justice Sequerah's exact phrasing — 'policy-centric' is paraphrase from FMT and Star reporting, not direct judgment text; Stage 3 explicitly cautioned against treating it as a quote, so the cards drop the single-quote marks while keeping the substance.
needs_verification: The Teh Cheng Poh MLJ pagination — Stage 2 cited [1979] 1 MLJ 50, Stage 3 used the same. The WLR citation [1979] 2 WLR 623 is consistent. The Federal Court editorial chambers index would be the cleanest resolution but is not strictly required for any card claim.
Whether Syed Iskandar has signalled an intention to seek Federal Court leave — not material to the cards as drafted.
source_notes: RESOLVED at Stage 3 (now applied): (1) High Court judge who dismissed the originating summons on 15 Feb 2024 is Wan Ahmad Farid Bin Wan Salleh (the current Chief Justice, cross-reference issue 1978). Not surfaced in any card as it would breach process-not-people discipline by drawing attention to one individual's role across two court levels. (2) The 1981 amendment Act number is A514, in force from 15 May 1981 — now in Card 2 and context. (3) Act A514 added Article 150(8) AND Article 150(9); 150(9) was the direct response to Teh Cheng Poh's ordinance-making reasoning, 150(8) the broader ouster. Card 2 sub now references both subclauses being added. (4) The 2021 emergency was effective from 11 January 2021 (Ordinance deemed in operation 11 Jan; gazetted 14 Jan; widely reported as 12 Jan, the announcement date). For card surfaces we omit the date and use 'in 2021' to avoid the 11/12 January discrepancy. (5) 1964-to-2026 is 62 years; 1964-to-2021 is 57 years. Card 4 big rewritten from 'in 60 years' to 'since 1964' (Stage 3 correction). (6) 'Architecture is sealed' (Card 7 view big) was an OVERCLAIM per Stage 3, since Article 150(3) Parliament annulment formally remains and Federal Court leave route is theoretically open. Rewritten to 'Only political checks remain.' (7) 'Suspended Parliament for seven months' (Card 1 hook sub) was legally loose per Stage 3; rewritten to 'Parliament did not sit for seven months' (factually accurate per Ordinance section 14 effect). (8) 'Policy-centric' (Card 3 sub) single quotes removed per Stage 3 caution against treating news paraphrase as direct quote. STAGE 4 (retired) and STAGE 5 (retired): None of the MY_REASONING flags imported. Verified Stage 4 framings (executive aggrandisement, political asymmetry of the 2020 refusal) are noted but not surfaced in cards because either (a) implicit in cards 1 + 5, or (b) would require characterising royal motivations and breach 3R Royalty discipline. Stage 5's structural-silence attacks on Conference of Rulers as unelected/closed-session are factually accurate but explicitly breach the 3R Royalty discipline set in Phase 1. Stage 5's audience-check critique of legal-register voice is noted but not actioned — the issue is constitutionally precise by editorial design, and adopting plain-language paraphrase would require dropping or loosening the Article 150(8)/150(3)/Indira Gandhi/Teh Cheng Poh references that constitute the entire structural point. Cross-issue: legal-register precision is the issue's purpose; we leave audience accessibility to the headline + hook + reframe + view, which use plain English ('issue it, or refuse it — is final', 'Two checks survive in text', 'Only political checks remain') even where the supporting cards do not.
revision_log: v2 Card 1 sub — REPHRASED (Stage 3 MISLEADING_FRAMING on 'suspended Parliament for seven months'): 'The challenge was to a refusal (October 2020). The same architecture covers issuance — including the 2021 Proclamation that suspended Parliament for seven months.' → 'The challenge was to a refusal (October 2020). The same architecture covers issuance — under which Parliament did not sit for seven months in 2021.' Verified against Emergency (Essential Powers) Ordinance 2021 P.U.(A) 12/2021 section 14, gazetted 14 Jan 2021.

v2 Card 2 sub — CORRECTED (Stage 3 OVERCLAIM on Teh Cheng Poh framing): 'Parliament added it in 1981 — a direct reversal of the Privy Council's 1979 Teh Cheng Poh ruling that emergency proclamations were reviewable.' → 'Parliament added 150(8) and (9) by Act A514 in May 1981, to oust review of emergencies after the Privy Council's 1979 Teh Cheng Poh ruling.' Verified against Court of Appeal Grounds of Decision 4 May 2026 paras [109]-[113] and Federal Constitution notes (Act A514, in force 15 May 1981).

v2 Card 3 sub — REPHRASED (Stage 3 caution on 'policy-centric' as paraphrase not quote): removed single quotes around 'policy-centric' so the phrase reads as our paraphrase rather than judicial direct quote. Substance unchanged.

v2 Card 4 big — CORRECTED (Stage 3 MISLEADING on '60 years' arithmetic): 'Five federal emergencies in 60 years (1964, 1966, 1969, 1977, 2021). One known refusal on record: October 2020.' → 'Five federal emergencies since 1964 (1964, 1966, 1969, 1977, 2021). One documented refusal on record: October 2020.' Also softened 'known' to 'documented' to address Stage 3 UNVERIFIED_DETAIL flag on the only-known-refusal claim.

v2 Card 4 sub — REPHRASED (Stage 3 UNVERIFIED_DETAIL on 'only documented check the system has ever produced'): 'With courts now out, it is the only documented check the system has produced.' → 'With courts now out, it is the only documented refusal the system has produced.' Refusal is verified; 'check' implied a broader constitutional claim Stage 2 and Stage 3 both flagged.

v2 Card 5 reframe — REPHRASED (Stage 2 OVERCLAIM + Stage 3 MISLEADING_FRAMING on Conference-of-Rulers-as-only-check): 'If neither courts nor citizens can question an emergency, the only check the Constitution still funds is the Conference of Rulers.' → 'Two checks survive in text: Parliament's Art. 150(3) annulment, and the Conference of Rulers. In 2021, Parliament did not sit while the emergency ran.' This is the highest-leverage correction in the synthesis. Verified against Federal Constitution Article 150(3) (primary text), Article 38, and Emergency (Essential Powers) Ordinance 2021 section 14. The new wording is also a stronger reframe per the playbook because it restructures (the system has paper checks; in practice one was disabled) rather than simply listing.

v2 Card 7 view — REPHRASED (Stage 3 OVERCLAIM on 'architecture is sealed'): 'Privy Council 1979: reviewable. 1981 Parliament: ousted. 2018 Federal Court: ouster invalid. 2026 Court of Appeal: emergencies excluded. The architecture is sealed.' (164 chars) → '1979 Privy Council: reviewable. 1981 Parliament: ousted. 2018 Federal Court: ouster invalid. 2026 Court of Appeal: emergencies excluded. Only political checks remain.' (165 chars). Stage 3 flag honoured: Article 150(3) parliamentary annulment formally remains and Federal Court leave route theoretically open.
v2 context — INTRODUCED (Stage 6 self-verified): added 'by Act A514' to the existing '1981' detail, verified against Federal Constitution notes (Act A514, in force 15 May 1981).

Cards 1 big, 3 big, 6 (analogy) — UNCHANGED from v1. Stage 2 and Stage 3 raised no objection to surface text. Stage 5's symmetry critique on the analogy is noted but not actioned — adopting Stage 5's framing would require characterising royal motivations, which breaches 3R Royalty discipline.

introduced_claims.claim: context: '...Article 150(8) — inserted by Act A514 in 1981 — ousts review of both issuance and refusal.'
introduced_claims.primary_source: Federal Constitution notes to Article 150 (SPRM PDF copy): Article 150(8) and (9) added by Act A514, paragraph 15(d), in force from 15 May 1981. https://www.sprm.gov.my/admin/files/sprm/assets/pdf/penguatkuasaan/perlembagaan-persekutuan-bi.pdf

introduced_claims.claim: card 1 sub: '...under which Parliament did not sit for seven months in 2021.'
introduced_claims.primary_source: Emergency (Essential Powers) Ordinance 2021 P.U.(A) 12/2021 section 14 (Parliament summoned, prorogued and dissolved on dates the Yang di-Pertuan Agong thought appropriate; in effect, Parliament did not sit during the relevant period). https://assets.theedgemarkets.com/pdf/pua_20210114_PUA12-Emergency%20%28Essential%20Powers%29%20Ordinance%202021.pdf

introduced_claims.claim: card 5 reframe: 'In 2021, Parliament did not sit while the emergency ran.'
introduced_claims.primary_source: Same Ordinance section 14 + Anwar Ibrahim v Kerajaan Malaysia [2021] 6 MLJ 68 (proclamation lapsed 1 Aug 2021). https://www.elaw.my/JE/01/JE_2021_38.pdf

Federal Constitution Article 150(3) (parliamentary annulment) + Article 38 (Conference of Rulers); applied as the verified-residual after Article 150(8) ouster confirmed by Court of Appeal 4 May 2026. Synthesised from Stage 3's explicit correction note and primary text.

synthesis_notes: OVER-INTEGRATION GUARD: Stage 2 flagged the Conference-of-Rulers-as-only-check claim as OVERCLAIM. Stage 5 (retired) flagged the Conference of Rulers itself as a structurally insufficient check (unelected, hereditary, closed-session). We chose Stage 2's correction (add Article 150(3)) over Stage 5's deeper attack (characterise the body's democratic deficit). Reason: Stage 2's correction is factual and reparative; Stage 5's attack would require characterising royal-institutional legitimacy, which the brief's Phase 1 3R Royalty discipline explicitly bars. The synthesis names the remaining checks accurately without ranking their democratic adequacy. COMPRESSION GUARD: Card 4 big shortened from 'in 60 years' to 'since 1964' loses a temporal anchor but gains accuracy. The shorter form does not drop any qualifier that was load-bearing — '60 years' was always rounded approximation. PUNCHY-BULLSHIT GUARD: 'The architecture is sealed' (Card 7) was tempting because of its closing rhythm; Stage 3 correctly flagged it as overclaim because Article 150(3) and Federal Court leave route formally remain. Rewrite 'Only political checks remain' preserves the rhythm while being defensible against a hostile reader who points to Article 150(3). AUDIENCE NOTE: Stage 5's audience-check critique (taxi driver in Ipoh would tune out) is noted but not actioned. The issue's editorial purpose is constitutional precision — adopting plain-language paraphrase would require dropping references to Article 150(8)/150(3), Indira Gandhi, Teh Cheng Poh, and Act A514, which together constitute the entire structural argument. We instead lean on the headline, hook, reframe and view to carry the plain-English version of the structural conclusion ('issue it, or refuse it — is final', 'Two checks survive in text', 'Parliament did not sit', 'Only political checks remain') while the fact cards carry the legal precision. This is the same architecture the Zahid-insults issue (1980) used.

_self_check.overclaim_check: Stage 2's OVERCLAIM flag on Conference-of-Rulers exclusivity is now FIXED (Card 5 reframe adds Article 150(3)). Stage 3's OVERCLAIM on 'architecture is sealed' is FIXED (Card 7 view 'Only political checks remain'). Stage 3's OVERCLAIM on Teh Cheng Poh framing is FIXED (Card 2 sub now references Act A514 adding 150(8) and (9) after Teh Cheng Poh, without overcompressing the case). No new overclaims introduced.
_self_check.underclaim_check: We do NOT soften the verified findings. Article 150(8) text is quoted exactly. The 4 May 2026 ruling is described as the Court of Appeal carving emergency proclamations out of the basic-structure protection — exactly what Stage 3 verified. Five emergencies and one documented refusal stated as the historical record. 'Only political checks remain' is the verified-harshest accurate framing after the Stage 3 hierarchy/temporal caveat is honoured.
_self_check.misleading_framing_check: Hook continues to lead with the dual scope (issuance and refusal) — verified by Khairuddin (2021, on issuance) and Syed Iskandar (2026, on refusal). Card 4 leads with the five-emergencies count to surface the asymmetry: many issuances, one documented refusal. Card 5 reframe now NAMES Article 150(3) and the Conference of Rulers as the two remaining textbook checks, then immediately notes Parliament did not sit during the 2021 emergency — the reader sees that one of the two paper checks was itself displaced by the emergency it was supposed to check. This is causal restructuring per the playbook's reframe rule. Analogy card explicitly notes both directions (issued 2021, refused 2020).
_self_check.unverified_detail_check: Every specific in v2 cards traces to a primary or near-primary source verified by Stage 2 or Stage 3. Article 150(8) text — Federal Constitution (Stage 3 primary). Act A514, in force 15 May 1981 — SPRM PDF copy of Federal Constitution (Stage 3 primary). Teh Cheng Poh [1979] 2 WLR 623 — Casemine + Court of Appeal Grounds at paras [109]-[113] (Stage 3 primary). Indira Gandhi [2018] 1 MLJ 545 — deolgill.com judgment text + Cambridge AJCL (Stage 3 primary). Five emergencies — BERNAMA + Vibes + Malaysiakini convergent. October 2020 refusal — Court of Appeal Grounds at para [3] + Malay Mail + BenarNews. 4 May 2026 ruling and panel — Court of Appeal Grounds (e-Kehakiman PDF) + FMT + Star. Article 150(3) — SPRM PDF copy of Federal Constitution. 2021 Emergency Ordinance section 14 — Edge Markets PDF copy of the gazetted text. 'Policy-centric' kept as paraphrase, not quote.
_self_check.3R_special_caution: Royalty: We continue NOT to characterise the Yang di-Pertuan Agong's preferences, motivations, or expected future conduct. The 25 October 2020 meeting is reported as a documented event (Stage 3 verified via Court of Appeal Grounds para [3] + Malay Mail + BenarNews). The Conference of Rulers is named as a constitutional body (Article 38) and historical mechanism (1983-84 episode), not characterised as more or less adequate. We use the formal title 'Yang di-Pertuan Agong' in card 1 and context and 'Agong' elsewhere — both standard usage. Religion and Race: not implicated.
_self_check.process_not_people_check: All sharp claims target the constitutional mechanic (Article 150(8) ouster, Article 150(3) annulment, basic-structure doctrine, the Teh Cheng Poh → 1981 → Indira Gandhi → 2026 chronology) or institutions (Court of Appeal, Federal Court, Privy Council, Conference of Rulers, Parliament). Muhyiddin is named for the historical fact of his October 2020 and January 2021 requests. The panel justices are named in source citations but not in card surface text. Syed Iskandar is named as the appellant in the context, not as a moral protagonist. Wan Ahmad Farid Bin Wan Salleh (HC judge resolved at Stage 3) is intentionally NOT named in any card — he is the current CJ and surfacing his prior role on this matter would invite a personalised narrative across two issues (1988 and 1978).
_self_check.anger_anxiety_check: Dominant emotion target: anxiety-of-precedent. The view card's chronological compression (1979 → 1981 → 2018 → 2026) lands the structural shift. The reframe is constructive in the playbook sense — it now names the remaining paper checks (Parliament's Art. 150(3) and the Conference of Rulers) and then surfaces the 2021 disabling, which is anxiety-of-precedent rather than anger-at-people. We avoid framing the Agong, the panel, or Parliament as villains.

sr_score_rationale: Synthesis raised from Stage 1 PA 87 to SR 88 because: (a) Stage 2 OVERCLAIM and Stage 3 MISLEADING_FRAMING on the Conference-of-Rulers reframe card both addressed via Article 150(3) inclusion — the single highest-leverage correction, defensible against the most likely hostile-reader pushback. (b) Stage 3 corrections on Teh Cheng Poh framing, 2021 'suspension' wording, '60 years' arithmetic, 'policy-centric' quotation marks, and 'architecture is sealed' overclaim all applied with explicit traceability. (c) Resolved needs_verification items (HC judge, Act A514 number and date) folded into context and Card 2 where load-bearing. (d) The HC judge's identity (current CJ) intentionally not surfaced — process-not-people decision documented. Capped at 88 because: (i) Justice Sequerah's 'policy-centric' is still paraphrase, not direct quote — Stage 3 cautioned but did not block; we kept the substance and dropped the quote marks. (ii) The 2021 emergency's 11-vs-12 January effective-date discrepancy is sidestepped by using 'in 2021' on card surfaces rather than reconciled in primary text. (iii) Stage 5 (retired) audience-check critique deliberately not actioned — defensible editorial choice but it does mean the cards will read as legal-register to a general audience. (iv) Stage 4 (retired) East Malaysia / B40 / gender perspective expansions deliberately not imported — a constitutional-precision issue cannot also be a class/regional-impact piece without breaking the bite-size budget.
